title: Chairman Babin Warns Risks to U.S. Science from Impending Government Shutdown
text:
(Washington, D.C.) – Today, House Science, Space, and Technology Committee Chairman Brian Babin released the following statement regarding the impending government shutdown.
“A government shutdown threatens to grind America’s science and technology enterprise to a halt,” Chairman Babin said. “Critical NASA missions with national security implications could be delayed, and cutting-edge research could be stalled. Our ability to prepare for extreme weather events and improve forecasting could also suffer, leaving Americans more vulnerable—especially as hurricane season continues. The House did its work to maintain government operations with a clean continuing resolution. Senate Democrats are driving us toward a shutdown that will put America’s leadership in space, science, and innovation at risk—plain and simple.”
